description: Needs to hear from others before deciding. Testimonials, case studies, and portfolio examples carry more weight than Zac's own claims. Often overlaps with betrayal-damaged archetype. Asks who else in their situation has signed with Stayful.
top_fears: being the first person to try something unproven
making a decision others have not validated
trusting claims with no external verification
top_questions: Can I speak to one of your existing owners?
How many properties do you manage?
Can you show me results from similar properties?
what_resonates: portfolio of 70 properties as proof of scale
Airbnb Superhost status as third-party validation
willingness to connect them with an existing owner
what_to_avoid: relying only on Zac's own claims
vague references to other owners without specifics
resisting requests to speak to existing clients
ideal_response_pattern: Provide third-party evidence then offer a specific owner reference if appropriate then show verified platform metrics then use portfolio size as credibility anchor
closing_approach: Offer to connect them with an existing owner in a similar situation. This single action often closes the deal for social-proof-dependent leads.
follow_up_strategy: Send platform profile links and specific comparable income examples. If owner reference was offered, follow up to confirm connection happened.

notable_leads.name: Karl
notable_leads.note: Also betrayal-damaged. Double archetype. Needs both social proof and trust rebuilding.

notable_leads.name: Vanessa
notable_leads.note: Also betrayal-damaged. Previous agent stole income. Testimonials particularly important.

notable_leads.name: Nathan Chadwick
notable_leads.note: Appeared alongside Don in entry 051. Social proof important for confidence.
